label: Planner-first is not the default in the live handler; planning only happens when routeAs, mergeModeHint, or targetAnchorId is already supplied.
The planner rewrite docs still center generate-context.js, but the live planner/apply path never invokes it and only offers in-handler full-auto and maintenance follow-ups.
Decision routing matches the docs, but insert-new-adr allocates ADR numbers from a local snapshot and relies on an in-process mutex, so cross-process saves can still collide on adr-NNN.
mergeModeHint is implemented but undocumented in the routing contract, and the code uses it only as a validator that rejects mismatches rather than as a routing selector.
The docs describe drop as scratch/pending-route-<hash>.json, but an explicit routeAs:"drop" resolves to scratch/pending-route-drop.json instead of a hash-specific refusal file.
The canonical route table uses research/research.md, but the memory_save schema and thrown allowlist error still advertise legacy research.md.

reason: The same eight categories appear in save.md, save_workflow.md, tool-schemas.ts, memory-save.ts, and content-router.ts; the drift is in gating, fallback wording, and a few target/UX details, not in the enum itself.

label: buildTarget() in content-router.ts is the route-table source of truth, while shouldPlanCanonicalSave in memory-save.ts is the real planner-vs-mutate gate; docs must match both surfaces, not just the route enum.
